mapping.a_element: fluctuations on top of a multi-component field configuration (the multi-vector condensate)
mapping.b_element: fluctuations of the temperature field decomposed into harmonic/spectral modes

mapping.a_element: weak-mixing vs strong-mixing regime of the coupled vector-field perturbations
mapping.b_element: harmonization of slow modes and broadening/decoherence of the DAHM phase distribution

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic notion of a multi-component field whose perturbations can mix, but the driving machinery differs: paper A is inflationary misalignment production of vector dark matter constrained by non-Gaussianity and isocurvature statistics, whereas paper B is transfer-operator spectral analysis (Ruelle--Pollicott resonances), extreme value theory, and data-adaptive harmonic modes detecting a metastable climate tipping transition. There is no shared governing process -- no critical slowing down / tipping in A and no misalignment / cosmological production in B -- so no coherent role-for-role mapping of the actual mechanism holds.
